reasoning: The recall was issued on April 24, 2025. The deadline for it to be marked 'Closed' on the FSIS website is July 1, 2025, a span of just 68 days. FSIS recalls typically remain 'Active' for several months or even years while the agency completes necessary effectiveness checks to ensure the product has been successfully removed from commerce. This is particularly true for frozen, long-shelf-life products (this product was produced starting in Nov 2023) and significant quantities (over 64,000 pounds). Therefore, it is highly unlikely the administrative status will officially change to 'Closed' within such a short two-month timeframe.
